llcFilesWith: New Hampshire Secretary of State, Corporation Division (trade name registration, Form TN-1, under RSA 349)
soleProprietorFilesWith: New Hampshire Secretary of State (the same statewide trade name registration applies to individuals and sole proprietors)
feeBasis: The Secretary of State fee to register a trade name is $50, and renewal is also $50.
renewalRule: A registration expires after five years (RSA 349:6) and must be re-registered; the Secretary of State mails renewal notices, and a name not renewed within 30 days of expiration is abandoned and freed (RSA 349:8).
protectionNote: New Hampshire folds registered trade names into the LLC name screen (RSA 304-C:32), so an active trade name blocks later entity names that are not distinguishable from it. Registration is a public claim rather than a trademark, and ownership ultimately turns on actual use.
searchNote: Registered trade names appear in the Secretary of State's QuickStart business name search.
notes: New Hampshire registers trade names statewide with the Secretary of State under RSA 349. LLCs and sole proprietors alike file a trade name registration for $50; unlike most states, the registration expires after five years and must be renewed for another $50, or the name is abandoned and freed. New Hampshire is stricter than most: a registered trade name is part of the RSA 304-C:32 entity-name screen, so it can block later LLC names, though registration is notice rather than a trademark.
